---
title: "Client Report - Famous Names"
subtitle: "Stretch Assignment - Elliot"
author: "Rebecca Hockman"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
---
```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)
```

```{python}
# Learn morea about Code Cells: https://quarto.org/docs/reference/cells/cells-jupyter.html

# Include and execute your code here
df = pl.read_csv("https://posit.byui.edu/content/0d43d66f-d3b7-4c43-8c36-52e3ed69e7f1/names_year_csv.csv")
```

Reproduce the chart Elliot using the data from the names_year.csv file. If, after a couple of hours, you can get the chart close, but not exactly the same, that is okay. You will learn more to the degree you push yourself.

```{python}


Elliot_df = (df.filter(pl.col("name") == "Elliot"))
 

plot = (
    ggplot(data=Elliot_df)
    + geom_line(aes(x='year', y='Total', color="name"), size=1)
    + geom_vline(xintercept=2002, linetype="dotted", color="red")
    + geom_vline(xintercept=1985, linetype="dotted", color="red")
    + geom_vline(xintercept=1982, linetype="dotted", color="red")
    + ggtitle(f"Elliot... what?")
    + xlab("Year")
    +ylab(" ")
    +scale_x_continuous(format="d")
    +scale_y_continuous(format="d")
    +scale_color_continuous(
        value = "blue"
    )
    +geom_text(x=1970, y=3000, label="E.T. Released", size=5)
    +geom_text(x=1988, y=3000, label="Second Release", size=5)
    +geom_text(x=2004, y=3000, label="Third Release", size=5)
    +scale_x_continuous(limits = [1950, 2025])
    + theme(
        plot_title=element_text(hjust=0),         
        plot_title_position="plot",                
        panel_background=element_rect(fill="lightgrey"), 
        panel_grid_major=element_line(color="white")
)
)

plot
```
